content.problemStatement:
Dragonado rarely gets stuck on a problem, unless of course, if it's a XOR Problem.

Dragonado while coming back from the Fruit Market, finds an array AAA of NNN elements. He wants to find 444 integers, say i1,i2,i3i_1,i_2,i_3i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã and i4i_4i4‚Äã, such that:


popcount(Ai1‚äïAi2)=popcount(Ai3‚äïAi4)\texttt{popcount}(A_{i_1} \oplus A_{i_2}) = \texttt{popcount}(A_{i_3} \oplus A_{i_4})popcount(Ai1‚Äã‚Äã‚äïAi2‚Äã‚Äã)=popcount(Ai3‚Äã‚Äã‚äïAi4‚Äã‚Äã)
1‚â§i1,i2,i3,i4‚â§N1 \le i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4 \le N1‚â§i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã,i4‚Äã‚â§N and  i1,i2,i3,i4i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã,i4‚Äã are pairwise distinct.
Here, popcount(x)\texttt{popcount}(x)popcount(x) denotes the number of set bits in the binary representation of xxx. Secondly, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Can you help Dragonado find four integers i1,i2,i3i_1, i_2, i_3i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã and i4i_4i4‚Äã which satisfy the given conditions?

Output ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no answer exists.

If multiple answers exist, output **any**.

Note: The input of this problem is large, so use fast input/output methods.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output i1,i2,i3,i4i_1,i_2,i_3,i_4i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã,i4‚Äã which satisfy the given conditions.


Output ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no numbers satisfying the conditions exist.
constraints: It is guaranteed that sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚ãÖ106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: 1,4,2,31, 4, 2, 31,4,2,3 is a valid answer because:

popcount(A1‚äïA4)=popcount(7)=3\texttt{popcount}(A_{1} \oplus A_{4}) = \texttt{popcount}(7) = 3popcount(A1‚Äã‚äïA4‚Äã)=popcount(7)=3
popcount(A2‚äïA3)=popcount(7)=3\texttt{popcount}(A_{2} \oplus A_{3}) = \texttt{popcount}(7) = 3popcount(A2‚Äã‚äïA3‚Äã)=popcount(7)=3

Therefore, popcount(Ai1‚äïAi2)=popcount(Ai3‚äïAi4)\texttt{popcount}(A_{i_1} \oplus A_{i_2}) = \texttt{popcount}(A_{i_3} \oplus A_{i_4})popcount(Ai1‚Äã‚Äã‚äïAi2‚Äã‚Äã)=popcount(Ai3‚Äã‚Äã‚äïAi4‚Äã‚Äã)
Test case-2: We can prove that no i1,i2,i3,i4i_1, i_2, i_3, i_4i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã,i4‚Äã exist that satisfy the given conditions.
